records.ety: [F. joint, fr. joindre, p. p. joint. See Join.]

defs: The place or part where two things or parts are joined or united; the union of two or more smooth or even surfaces admitting of a close-fitting or junction; junction; as, a joint between two pieces of timber; a joint in a pipe.
A joining of two things or parts so as to admit of motion; an articulation, whether movable or not; a hinge; as, the knee joint; a node or joint of a stem; a ball and socket joint. See Articulation.
quotes: A scaly gauntlet now, with joints of steel, Must glove this hand.
To tear thee joint by joint.

defs: The part or space included between two joints, knots, nodes, or articulations; as, a joint of cane or of a grass stem; a joint of the leg.
Any one of the large pieces of meat, as cut into portions by the butcher for roasting.
A plane of fracture, or divisional plane, of a rock transverse to the stratification.
The space between the adjacent surfaces of two bodies joined and held together, as by means of cement, mortar, etc.; as, a thin joint.
The means whereby the meeting surfaces of pieces in a structure are secured together.
A projecting or retreating part in something; any irregularity of line or surface, as in a wall.
A narrow piece of scenery used to join together two flats or wings of an interior setting.

a disreputable establishment, or a place of low resort, as for smoking opium; -- also used for a commercial establishment, implying a less than impeccable reputation, but often in jest; as, talking about a high-class joint is an oxymoron.
a marijuana cigarette.
prison; -- used with “the”.

records.ety: [F., p. p. of joindre. See Join.]

defs: Joined; united; combined; concerted; as, joint action.
Involving the united activity of two or more; done or produced by two or more working together.
quotes: I read this joint effusion twice over.

defs: United, joined, or sharing with another or with others; not solitary in interest or action; holding in common with an associate, or with associates; acting together; as, joint heir; joint creditor; a joint bank account; joint debtor, etc.
Shared by, or affecting two or more; held in common; as, joint property; a joint bond.
quotes: A joint burden laid upon us all.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Jointed; p. pr. & vb. n. Jointing.]

defs: To unite by a joint or joints; to fit together; to prepare so as to fit together; as, to joint boards.
quotes: Pierced through the yielding planks of jointed wood.

defs: To join; to connect; to unite; to combine.
To provide with a joint or joints; to articulate.
quotes: The fingers are jointed together for motion.

defs: To separate the joints; of; to divide at the joint or joints; to disjoint; to cut up into joints, as meat.
To fit as if by joints; to coalesce as joints do; as, the stones joint, neatly.
